features.title: Product catalog browsing (2-level categories)
features.description:
Customers can browse the product catalog organized by a hierarchical category system with a maximum of two nesting levels (e.g., "Electronics > Headphones").

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-1: Browse products by category*
- Categories are displayed in sidebar or top navigation with product counts showing how many products belong to each category.
- The category hierarchy supports two-level nesting: root categories and one level of subcategories.
- Clicking a category shows all products belonging to that category.
- Product listings use offset-based pagination with 20 items per page.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Category**: id, parent_id (FK, nullable — null for root categories), name, slug (unique), sort_order. The parent_id self-reference enables the two-level hierarchy. Maximum nesting depth is strictly 2 levels.
- **Product**: id, category_id (FK), name, slug (unique), description (rich text), base_price, is_active, total_units_sold. Only active products (is_active = true) appear on the storefront.
- **ProductImage**: id, product_id (FK), url, alt_text, sort_order. Used to display product thumbnails in listing views.
- **ProductVariant**: id, product_id (FK), name, sku (unique), price_override (nullable), stock_quantity. Variant stock quantities determine whether a product is shown as in-stock.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Products use a soft-delete pattern via the is_active flag — deactivated products are hidden from the storefront but preserved in the database and in existing orders.
- Only active products appear in category browsing views.
- Rich-text product descriptions must be sanitized before rendering.
- The catalog is designed to support up to ~10,000 products.
- This is a single-region (US), single-currency (USD) store.

features.title: Product search (PostgreSQL full-text)
features.description:
Customers can search for products across the entire store using a search bar available on every page. Search uses full-text search capabilities to match against product names and descriptions.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-2: Search for products*
- A search bar is present on every page of the storefront.
- Search queries match against product name and product description fields.
- Results are ranked by relevance using full-text search.
- When no results are found, a friendly "no results" message is displayed along with suggestions (e.g., check spelling, try different keywords, or browse categories).
- Search results are paginated using offset-based pagination with 20 items per page.
- Only active products (is_active = true) appear in search results.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Product**: id, category_id (FK), name, slug (unique), description (rich text), base_price, is_active, total_units_sold. The name and description fields are the searchable fields.
- **ProductImage**: Used for displaying thumbnails in search results.
- **ProductVariant**: Used to determine stock availability shown in search results.
- **Category**: Referenced for showing category breadcrumbs or labels on search result items.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Search is limited to product name and description — it does not search by SKU, category name, or review text.
- Only active products appear in search results.
- The search must perform well for a catalog of up to ~10,000 products.
- API response time target is <500ms at p95.

features.title: Filtering & sorting
features.description:
Customers can refine product listings using filters and sorting options. Filters and sorts apply to category browsing and search results, and update the displayed products without a full page reload.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-3: Filter and sort product listings*
- **Filters available:**
  - Price range (minimum and maximum price)
  - Minimum star rating (based on average review rating)
  - In-stock only (hides products where all variants have zero stock)
- **Sort options:**
  - Price: low to high
  - Price: high to low
  - Newest arrivals (most recently created products first)
  - Most popular (sorted by total_units_sold descending)
- Active filters are shown as removable chips/tags, each individually removable.
- A "Clear all" option removes all active filters at once.
- Filter and sort changes update the product listing without a full page reload (client-side state management).
- Filters and sorts work in combination (e.g., filter by price range AND sort by newest).
- Pagination resets to page 1 when filters or sorts change.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Product**: base_price (for price filtering/sorting), is_active, total_units_sold (for popularity sorting), created_at (for newest arrivals sorting).
- **ProductVariant**: stock_quantity (for in-stock filtering — a product is in-stock if any variant has stock_quantity > 0), price_override (effective price is price_override if set, otherwise product base_price).
- **Review**: rating (1–5) aggregated per product to compute average star rating for the rating filter.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Filtering and sorting happen via API queries, with client-side state management for the UI.
- Product listings use offset-based pagination at 20 items per page.
- Price filtering should account for variant price overrides (the effective display price).
- No saved/bookmarked filter presets in v1.

features.title: Product detail pages (gallery, variants, stock)
features.description:
Each product has a dedicated detail page displaying comprehensive product information including an image gallery, variant selection, pricing, stock status, reviews summary, and an add-to-cart action.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-4: View product detail page*
- **Image gallery**: Displays product images with thumbnail navigation. Images are ordered by sort_order. Up to 10 images per product.
- **Product information**: Full product name, rich-text description (sanitized before rendering), category breadcrumb (e.g., "Electronics > Headphones"), base price.
- **Variant selection**: Dropdown or button group to select a product variant. Selecting a variant updates the displayed price (uses price_override if set, otherwise base_price) and stock status.
- **Stock status**: Clearly indicates whether the selected variant is in stock or out of stock based on stock_quantity.
- **"Add to Cart" button**: Adds the product with the selected variant to the cart. The button is disabled when the selected variant is out of stock (stock_quantity = 0). A variant must be selected before adding to cart.
- **Reviews summary**: Displays the average rating (1–5 stars) and total review count for the product.
- **Rating distribution**: Shows the breakdown of ratings (how many 1-star, 2-star, etc.).

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Product**: id, category_id (FK), name, slug (unique), description (rich text), base_price, is_active.
- **ProductImage**: id, product_id (FK), url, alt_text, sort_order. Images served via CDN.
- **ProductVariant**: id, product_id (FK), name, sku (unique), price_override (nullable — falls back to base_price if null), stock_quantity.
- **Category**: id, parent_id, name, slug. Used for breadcrumb navigation.
- **Review**: rating, aggregated to compute average rating and review count displayed on the detail page.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Only active products (is_active = true) are accessible on the storefront; inactive products should return a 404 or redirect.
- Rich-text descriptions must be sanitized with DOMPurify before rendering.
- Images are stored in S3 and served via CloudFront CDN — max 10 images, max 5 MB each.
- Initial page load target is <3s; subsequent navigation <1s.
- No product comparison or recommendation features in v1.

features.title: Product variants (flat model)
features.description:
Products support a flat variant model where each product can have multiple variants, each with its own name, SKU, optional price override, and independent stock quantity.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

Variants are referenced across multiple user stories:

*From US-4 (Product detail page):*
- Variant selection on product pages updates the displayed price and stock status.
- If a variant has a price_override, that price is shown; otherwise, the product's base_price is used.
- Stock status is per-variant (each variant has its own stock_quantity).

*From US-10 (Shopping cart):*
- A variant must be selected before a product can be added to cart.
- The same product with the same variant increments quantity rather than creating a duplicate line item.
- Cart quantity for a line item is capped at the variant's available stock_quantity.

*From US-11 (Checkout):*
- Stock is validated at both add-to-cart and checkout time.
- Stock decrement is atomic per variant at payment time.

*From US-17 (Admin product management):*
- Admins can create and manage flat variants for each product.
- Each variant has: name, SKU (globally unique), optional price override, and stock quantity.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **ProductVariant**: id, product_id (FK), name, sku (unique across all variants), price_override (nullable — when null, the product's base_price applies), stock_quantity (non-negative integer).
- **Product**: id, base_price. The base_price serves as the default price when a variant has no price_override.
- **CartItem**: variant_id (FK) — links a cart item to a specific variant.
- **OrderItem**: variant_id (FK, nullable), variant_name (snapshot of variant name at purchase time), unit_price (snapshot of effective price at purchase time).

**Scope Constraints:**
- The variant model is "flat" — each variant is a simple name/SKU/price/stock record. There are no multi-dimensional variant attributes (e.g., no separate "size" and "color" dimensions).
- SKUs must be globally unique across all variants of all products.
- Stock quantity must never go negative — atomic decrement uses a conditional update: only succeeds if stock_quantity >= requested quantity.
- Price override is optional; null means the product's base_price is the effective price for that variant.
- No variant-specific images in v1 (images are at the product level).

features.title: User registration & login (JWT, lockout, password reset)
features.description:
Users can create accounts, log in, log out, and reset forgotten passwords. Authentication uses JWT tokens stored in httpOnly cookies with security measures including rate limiting and account lockout.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-6: Register an account*
- Registration form fields: email, password, first name, last name.
- Email must be unique across all users.
- Password requirements: minimum 8 characters, must contain a mix of letters and numbers.
- A password strength indicator is shown during registration.
- Upon successful registration, the user is automatically logged in (JWT cookies are set immediately).

*US-7: Log in and log out*
- Login form fields: email and password.
- On successful login, the server issues an access token (15-minute expiry) and a refresh token (7-day expiry), both stored in httpOnly, secure, sameSite=strict cookies.
- **Brute-force protection**: After 5 failed login attempts within 15 minutes, the account is locked out for 30 minutes.
- **"Forgot password?" flow**: User enters email → receives a password reset email with a single-use link that expires after 1 hour → clicks link → sets a new password.
- **Logout**: Clears cookies and blacklists the refresh token in Redis so it cannot be reused.
- **Token refresh**: When the access token expires, the client can use the refresh token to obtain a new access token without re-entering credentials.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **User**: id, email (unique), password_hash, first_name, last_name, phone, role (customer | admin), is_active. Password is hashed with bcrypt (12 salt rounds). The is_active flag enables soft-delete / account deactivation.

**Scope Constraints:**
- JWT tokens must never be stored in localStorage — httpOnly cookies only.
- Access tokens expire in 15 minutes; refresh tokens expire in 7 days.
- Refresh tokens are stored/validated in Redis for blacklisting on logout.
- Rate limiting on authentication endpoints is Redis-backed.
- The initial admin account is created via a database seed script, not through the registration flow.
- Out of scope for v1: social/OAuth login, two-factor authentication (2FA), email verification on registration, account deletion / GDPR export.
- Deactivated users (is_active = false) are forced to logout on their next request.

features.title: User profile & addresses
features.description:
Authenticated users can manage their profile information and maintain up to 5 shipping addresses. An account dashboard provides an overview of the user's activity.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-8: Manage account profile*
- **Profile editing**: Users can edit their first name, last name, and phone number. Email is read-only in v1 (cannot be changed).
- **Address management**:
  - Users can store up to 5 shipping addresses.
  - Each address has a label (e.g., "Home", "Office"), street, city, state, zip code, and country.
  - One address can be marked as the default shipping address.
  - Addresses can be added, edited, and deleted.
- **Account dashboard** (at `/account`):
  - Shows the user's latest/most recent order summary.
  - Displays the count of saved addresses.
  - Displays the count of wishlist items.
  - Shows a profile completeness indicator.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **User**: id, email (unique, read-only), password_hash, first_name, last_name, phone, role, is_active.
- **Address**: id, user_id (FK), label, street, city, state, zip_code, country, is_default. Maximum 5 addresses per user. Exactly one address can be marked is_default = true per user.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Email cannot be changed in v1.
- Maximum of 5 addresses per user — attempting to add a 6th should show an error.
- When an address is used in an order, the order stores a JSON snapshot of the address, so editing or deleting an address does not affect existing orders.
- Admins cannot edit a user's profile details — they can only change role and active status.
- Out of scope for v1: account deletion, GDPR data export, email change.

features.title: Shopping cart (guest + authenticated, merge)
features.description:
Both guest visitors and authenticated users can add products to a shopping cart. Guest carts are stored in the browser's localStorage. When a guest logs in or registers, their localStorage cart is merged with any existing server-side cart.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-10: Manage shopping cart*
- **Adding to cart**:
  - A variant must be selected before a product can be added to the cart.
  - Adding the same product+variant combination increments the quantity instead of creating a duplicate line item.
  - Cart icon in the header/navigation shows the total item count.
  - A toast notification is shown when an item is added to the cart.
- **Cart page displays for each item**:
  - Product image (thumbnail)
  - Product name
  - Variant name
  - Unit price (effective price: variant's price_override or product's base_price)
  - Quantity selector (capped at the variant's current stock_quantity)
  - Line total (unit price × quantity)
  - Remove button to delete the item from cart
- **Cart summary**:
  - Subtotal (sum of all line totals)
  - Estimated tax (based on the flat tax rate from StoreSetting)
  - Total (subtotal + estimated tax)
- **Empty cart state**: Displays a "Continue Shopping" link/button.
- **Guest cart behavior**: Stored in browser localStorage. Persists across page reloads but is device/browser-specific.
- **Cart merge on login/registration**: When a guest with items in localStorage logs in or registers, the localStorage cart is merged with their server-side cart. For duplicate product+variant combinations, the higher quantity wins. After merge, the localStorage cart is cleared.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Cart**: id, user_id (FK). One cart per authenticated user. Created automatically when needed.
- **CartItem**: id, cart_id (FK), product_id (FK), variant_id (FK, nullable), quantity. Represents a single line item in the cart.
- **Product**: Referenced for product name, base_price, is_active status.
- **ProductVariant**: Referenced for variant name, price_override, stock_quantity (to cap cart quantity).
- **ProductImage**: Referenced for thumbnail display in cart.
- **StoreSetting**: key-value pairs including tax_rate used for the estimated tax calculation in the cart summary.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Guest carts live entirely in the browser (localStorage) — no server-side guest cart storage.
- Server-side carts exist only for authenticated users.
- Cart quantities are validated against current stock at display time and again at checkout.
- If a product or variant becomes inactive or out of stock after being added to cart, it should be indicated to the user.
- Cart does not store prices — prices are always calculated from current product/variant data (except in orders where they are snapshotted).
- No saved carts, shared carts, or cart expiration in v1.

features.title: Multi-step checkout (Stripe)
features.description:
Authenticated users can complete a purchase through a multi-step checkout flow that collects shipping information, shipping method selection, and payment via Stripe. The checkout process validates stock, processes payment, creates the order, and decrements inventory atomically.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-11: Complete checkout* (requires authentication — guests must log in or register first)

The checkout consists of 4 sequential steps:

1. **Shipping Address**:
   - User selects from their saved addresses or enters a new address.
   - Option to save a new address to their profile for future use.
   - Address is validated (all required fields filled).

2. **Shipping Method**:
   - Two options: Standard (5–7 business days) and Express (2–3 business days).
   - Each has a fixed price (stored in StoreSetting).
   - The cheapest option (Standard) is pre-selected by default.

3. **Payment**:
   - Payment is collected via Stripe Elements (card form embedded in the page).
   - Card data never touches the application server — it goes directly to Stripe.
   - On payment failure, a clear error message is shown with the option to retry.
   - Uses Stripe Payment Intents API.

4. **Confirmation**:
   - Displays: order number, list of items purchased, shipping address, shipping method, and a full price breakdown (subtotal, shipping cost, tax amount, total).
   - Shows estimated delivery date based on the selected shipping method.

**Stock validation and atomic decrement:**
- Stock is validated when items are added to cart AND again at checkout time before payment.
- Upon successful payment, stock is decremented atomically using a conditional update that only succeeds if sufficient stock exists (stock_quantity >= requested quantity).
- If stock is insufficient at checkout, the user is informed and the order is not placed.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Order**: id, user_id (FK), order_number (unique, auto-generated), status (initially "paid"), shipping_address (JSON snapshot of the address at order time — not a FK), shipping_method ("standard" | "express"), shipping_cost, subtotal, tax_amount, total, tracking_number (null initially), estimated_delivery_date, stripe_payment_intent_id, admin_notes.
- **OrderItem**: id, order_id (FK), product_id (FK), variant_id (FK, nullable), product_name (snapshot), variant_name (snapshot), unit_price (snapshot of effective price at purchase), quantity, line_total. All name and price fields are snapshots frozen at purchase time.
- **OrderStatusLog**: id, order_id (FK), old_status (null for initial), new_status ("paid"), changed_by (FK — the user), timestamp. Records the initial order creation.
- **Address**: Used to populate the shipping address selector.
- **StoreSetting**: Contains standard_shipping_price, express_shipping_price, and tax_rate values.
- **ProductVariant**: stock_quantity is decremented atomically upon successful payment.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Checkout requires authentication — unauthenticated users must log in or register first.
- Card payment data must never touch the application server — Stripe Elements / Stripe.js only.
- Shipping address on orders is a JSON snapshot, not a foreign key — it survives address edits/deletes.
- OrderItem fields (product_name, variant_name, unit_price) are snapshots at purchase time so historical orders remain accurate even if products change.
- The order status after successful payment is "paid".
- Stripe webhook integration is used to confirm payment status.
- Out of scope for v1: promo codes/coupons/discounts, saved payment methods, digital wallets, order editing after placement, subscriptions.

features.title: Flat tax calculation
features.description:
Tax is calculated as a single flat percentage rate applied to the order subtotal. The tax rate is configurable via a store setting.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-12: Tax calculation*
- A single flat tax rate (percentage) is applied to all orders.
- The tax rate is stored as a configurable value in the StoreSetting table.
- Tax is calculated as: tax_amount = subtotal × (tax_rate / 100), rounded to 2 decimal places.
- Tax is displayed in the cart summary as "estimated tax" and in the checkout confirmation as the final tax amount.
- Tax amount is stored on the Order record.
- Total = subtotal + shipping_cost + tax_amount.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **StoreSetting**: key-value store. The key "tax_rate" holds the flat tax percentage (e.g., "8.5" for 8.5%). Also contains "standard_shipping_price" and "express_shipping_price".
- **Order**: subtotal, tax_amount, shipping_cost, total. Tax amount is computed and stored at order creation time.

**Scope Constraints:**
- US only, USD only — no multi-region or multi-currency tax rules.
- Single flat rate — no per-state, per-category, or per-product tax rates.
- The tax rate can be updated by admins via the settings API, but changes only affect future orders (existing orders retain their stored tax_amount).
- Out of scope for v1: third-party tax calculation services, tax-exempt customers, tax reporting.

features.title: Order management (customer)
features.description:
Authenticated customers can view their order history and detailed information about each order, including items purchased, shipping details, payment summary, and status tracking.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-13: View order history and details*
- **Order history list** (at `/account/orders`):
  - Displays: order number, order date, item count, total amount, and a color-coded status badge.
  - Order statuses: pending, paid, processing, shipped, delivered, cancelled, refunded.
  - Each status has a distinct color for easy visual identification.
  - Orders are listed in reverse chronological order (newest first).
  - Paginated using offset-based pagination.
- **Order detail page** (at `/account/orders/:id`):
  - **Line items**: product name, variant name, unit price, quantity, line total — all from the order snapshot, not current product data.
  - **Shipping information**: shipping address (from JSON snapshot), shipping method, tracking number (if available), estimated delivery date.
  - **Payment summary**: subtotal, shipping cost, tax amount, total.
  - **Status timeline**: visual chronological display of all status changes with timestamps.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Order**: id, user_id (FK), order_number (unique), status, shipping_address (JSON snapshot), shipping_method, shipping_cost, subtotal, tax_amount, total, tracking_number, estimated_delivery_date, stripe_payment_intent_id, admin_notes.
- **OrderItem**: id, order_id (FK), product_id (FK), variant_id (FK, nullable), product_name (snapshot), variant_name (snapshot), unit_price (snapshot), quantity, line_total.
- **OrderStatusLog**: id, order_id (FK), old_status, new_status, changed_by (FK), timestamp. Used to build the status timeline.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Customers can only view their own orders (user_id must match the authenticated user).
- All product/variant names and prices shown in order details come from the order snapshots, not from current product data.
- The shipping address is a JSON snapshot — it reflects what was provided at checkout, regardless of any subsequent address changes.
- Admin notes on orders are not visible to customers.
- Out of scope for v1: email notifications for order status changes, invoice/receipt PDF generation.

features.title: Order cancellation & refund
features.description:
Customers can cancel eligible orders, which triggers an automatic refund through Stripe and restores the reserved stock.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-15: Cancel an order*
- A "Cancel Order" action is available on orders with status "paid" or "processing" only.
- Orders with status "shipped", "delivered", "cancelled", or "refunded" cannot be cancelled by the customer.
- When a customer cancels an order:
  1. The order status is changed to "cancelled".
  2. An automatic full refund is initiated via Stripe (using the stored stripe_payment_intent_id).
  3. Stock quantities for all order items are restored (incremented back) on the corresponding ProductVariant records.
  4. The status change is logged in OrderStatusLog with the customer's identity and timestamp.
- The customer receives confirmation that the cancellation was successful.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Order**: id, status, stripe_payment_intent_id (used to issue the Stripe refund), user_id.
- **OrderItem**: product_id, variant_id, quantity — used to determine which variants need stock restored and by how much.
- **ProductVariant**: stock_quantity — incremented by the order item quantity upon cancellation.
- **OrderStatusLog**: id, order_id (FK), old_status ("paid" or "processing"), new_status ("cancelled"), changed_by (FK — the customer), timestamp.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Cancellation is only available for orders in "paid" or "processing" status.
- Refunds are full refunds only — no partial cancellations or partial refunds by the customer in v1.
- Admins can also issue refunds on any paid or completed order (see Admin Order Management — feature #19).
- Stock restoration must be reliable — if the Stripe refund fails, the cancellation should not proceed (or should be handled gracefully).
- Out of scope for v1: partial refunds by customers, returns and exchanges, order editing after placement.

features.title: Reorder
features.description:
Customers can quickly reorder items from a past order, adding them back to the current shopping cart.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-14: Reorder from past orders*
- A "Reorder" button/action is available on the order detail page or order history list.
- Clicking "Reorder" adds all items from the past order back to the current shopping cart, matching the exact product and variant combinations from the original order.
- For each item in the original order:
  - If the product and variant are still active and in stock, the item is added to the cart with the original quantity (capped at current available stock).
  - If the product or variant is no longer available (inactive or deleted), that item is skipped.
- After the reorder action, the user is notified about:
  - Which items were successfully added to the cart.
  - Which items were skipped and why (e.g., "Product X is no longer available", "Variant Y is out of stock").
- If no items from the order can be reordered, an appropriate message is shown.
- Reordered items follow the same cart rules: same product+variant increments quantity, quantity capped at stock.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Order**: id, user_id — to verify the order belongs to the customer.
- **OrderItem**: product_id (FK), variant_id (FK, nullable), quantity — the original order items to be re-added.
- **Product**: is_active — checked to determine if the product is still available.
- **ProductVariant**: id, stock_quantity, is related to Product — checked to determine if the variant still exists and has stock.
- **Cart / CartItem**: The destination for reordered items.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Prices in the new cart reflect current prices, not the prices from the original order.
- Reorder uses the product_id and variant_id from OrderItem to look up current products — it does not rely on snapshot names.
- Items that cannot be reordered are silently skipped with a notification — the reorder action does not fail entirely.
- This is a P2 (lower priority) feature within the MVP.

features.title: Product reviews
features.description:
Authenticated customers who have purchased a product can submit reviews with star ratings and text. Reviews are displayed on the product detail page and contribute to the product's average rating.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-5: View and submit product reviews*
- **Viewing reviews:**
  - Reviews are displayed on the product detail page.
  - Each review shows: reviewer's name, star rating (1–5), review text, and date.
  - A rating distribution summary is shown at the top (breakdown of how many 1-star, 2-star, 3-star, 4-star, and 5-star reviews).
  - Reviews are offset-paginated (using the default page size).
- **Submitting reviews:**
  - Only authenticated users can submit reviews.
  - Only verified purchasers (users who have a completed/delivered order containing the product) can submit reviews.
  - Review form includes: star rating (1–5, required) and text (required).
  - Reviews are published immediately upon submission — there is no moderation queue.
- **Editing and deleting reviews:**
  - Customers can edit their own reviews. Edited reviews display an "edited" label.
  - Customers can delete their own reviews.
  - Administrators can delete any review (but cannot edit other users' reviews).

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Review**: id, product_id (FK), user_id (FK), rating (integer 1–5), text, is_edited (boolean, set to true when edited), created_at, updated_at.
- **Product**: Reviews are aggregated to compute average rating and review count displayed on product cards and detail pages.
- **User**: first_name, last_name — used to display reviewer name. user_id is the foreign key linking the review to its author.
- **Order / OrderItem**: Used to verify that the reviewer has actually purchased the product (verified purchaser check).

**Scope Constraints:**
- One review per customer per product (a customer cannot submit multiple reviews for the same product).
- Reviews are published immediately — no admin moderation queue in v1.
- The verified purchaser check requires a completed order (delivered status) containing the specific product.
- Review text should be plain text (no rich text/HTML in reviews).
- Admin can delete any review but cannot edit reviews they did not author.
- Reviews survive product category changes — moving a product preserves all its reviews.
- Out of scope for v1: review images/photos, review helpfulness votes, review replies.

features.title: Wishlist
features.description:
Authenticated users can save products to a wishlist for future reference, accessible from the product catalog and account area.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-9: Manage wishlist*
- A heart icon toggle is available on product cards (in listings/search results) and on the product detail page.
- Clicking the heart icon adds the product to the wishlist; clicking again removes it.
- The heart icon visually indicates whether a product is already in the user's wishlist (filled vs. outline).
- Out-of-stock products can remain on the wishlist — they are displayed with an "out of stock" label rather than being automatically removed.
- The wishlist is accessible from the user's account area (at `/account/wishlist` or similar).
- The wishlist page displays product cards with current product information (name, price, image, stock status).
- Users can remove items from the wishlist.
- The account dashboard shows the wishlist item count.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Wishlist**: user_id (FK), product_id (FK). This is a join table — the composite key (user_id, product_id) ensures a product can only appear once per user's wishlist.
- **Product**: id, name, slug, base_price, is_active. Referenced to display current product information on the wishlist page.
- **ProductImage**: Used for product thumbnails on the wishlist page.
- **ProductVariant**: stock_quantity — used to determine and display stock status on wishlist items.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Wishlist is per-product, not per-variant (a user wishlists a product, not a specific variant).
- Only authenticated users can use the wishlist — guests cannot save wishlist items.
- Deactivated products may remain on the wishlist but should be clearly indicated if they are no longer available.
- No "notify when back in stock" functionality in v1.
- No guest wishlist or wishlist merge on login in v1.
- This is a P2 (lower priority) feature within the MVP.

features.title: Admin dashboard (KPIs, charts)
features.description:
Administrators have access to a dashboard showing key store performance metrics and charts for a quick overview of business activity.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-16: View store performance*
- **KPI cards** (computed for the last 30 days):
  - Total revenue (sum of order totals for completed orders)
  - Total number of orders
  - Average Order Value (AOV = total revenue / total orders)
  - Registered customer count (total users with role = customer)
- **Daily revenue chart**:
  - Bar or line chart showing revenue per day for the last 30 days.
  - Built with Recharts charting library.
- **Top 5 best-selling products**:
  - List of the 5 products with the highest total_units_sold.
  - Shows product name and units sold.
- **5 most recent orders**:
  - Shows order number, customer name/email, total, status, and date.
  - Quick link to the full order detail in the admin order management section.
- **Data freshness**: All data is fetched on page load — there are no real-time updates or auto-refresh.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Order**: status, total, created_at — aggregated for revenue, order count, and AOV calculations. Only orders with appropriate statuses (paid, processing, shipped, delivered) count toward revenue.
- **User**: role — used to count registered customers.
- **Product**: total_units_sold — used for the best-selling products list.
- **OrderItem**: Potentially used for more granular sales data.

**Scope Constraints:**
- The dashboard is accessible only to users with the admin role.
- KPIs cover the last 30 days (rolling window from the current date).
- No real-time updates — data is loaded once on page visit.
- No configurable date ranges in v1 — always the last 30 days.
- No data export or downloadable reports in v1.
- Out of scope: analytics beyond these basic KPIs, conversion rates, funnel analysis.

features.title: Admin product management
features.description:
Administrators can create, edit, and manage products including their images, variants, pricing, and active/inactive status through a back-office interface.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-17: Manage products*
- **Product list table**:
  - Columns: thumbnail image, product name, category, base price, total stock (sum of all variant stock quantities), active/inactive status.
  - Search products by name.
  - Filter by category and by active/inactive status.
  - Sort by name, price, or creation date.
  - Paginated with 25 rows per page (admin table pagination).
- **Create / Edit product form**:
  - Fields: name, slug (auto-generated from name, but editable), rich-text description (with a rich text editor), category (dropdown), base price, active/inactive toggle.
  - Slug must be unique across all products.
- **Image management**:
  - Up to 10 images per product, maximum 5 MB each.
  - Images uploaded directly to S3 via presigned URLs (not streamed through the API server).
  - Drag-and-drop reorder to set display order (sort_order).
  - Alt text field for each image.
  - Images can be added and removed.
- **Variant management** (inline within product form):
  - Flat variants: each has a name, SKU (globally unique), optional price override, and stock quantity.
  - Variants can be added, edited, and removed.
- **Soft-delete**:
  - Products are deactivated (is_active = false), not permanently deleted.
  - Deactivating a product hides it from the storefront but preserves it in the database and in existing orders.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Product**: id, category_id (FK), name, slug (unique), description (rich text), base_price, is_active, total_units_sold, created_at, updated_at.
- **ProductImage**: id, product_id (FK), url, alt_text, sort_order. Images stored in S3, served via CloudFront.
- **ProductVariant**: id, product_id (FK), name, sku (unique), price_override (nullable), stock_quantity.
- **Category**: id, name — used for category selection dropdown.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Only admin users can access product management.
- Products are never permanently deleted — soft-delete only via is_active flag.
- Image upload uses presigned S3 URLs — the image binary never passes through the API server.
- Rich-text descriptions must be sanitized before rendering on the storefront.
- Slug auto-generation from name but must remain editable for SEO purposes.
- Out of scope for v1: bulk product import/export, in-browser image cropping, variant-specific images.

features.title: Admin category management
features.description:
Administrators can create, edit, reorder, and manage the product category hierarchy used to organize the storefront catalog.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-18: Manage categories*
- **Category tree view**: Displays all categories in a visual tree structure showing the parent-child hierarchy.
- **Maximum 2 nesting levels**: Root categories and one level of subcategories. No deeper nesting is allowed.
- **Create categories**: Admins can create new root categories or subcategories under an existing root category.
- **Edit categories**: Admins can edit a category's name, slug, and parent assignment (move a subcategory to a different root, or promote a subcategory to root, etc.).
- **Drag-and-drop reorder**: Categories can be reordered within their level using drag-and-drop, which updates the sort_order field.
- **Deletion protection**: A category cannot be deleted if it has products assigned to it. Admins must first reassign all products to a different category before the category can be removed.
- **Data integrity**: Moving a product to a different category preserves all of the product's reviews, ratings, and order history.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Category**: id, parent_id (FK, nullable — null for root categories), name, slug (unique), sort_order. The parent_id self-reference creates the hierarchy. sort_order determines display ordering within the same level.
- **Product**: category_id (FK) — the foreign key linking products to categories.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Maximum hierarchy depth is strictly 2 levels (root → subcategory). The system must prevent creating a third level.
- Category slugs must be unique across all categories.
- Categories are not soft-deleted — they can be permanently removed, but only when they have no assigned products.
- Moving products between categories does not affect any product data (reviews, ratings, orders, images, variants all remain intact).
- Only admin users can access category management.

features.title: Admin order management
features.description:
Administrators can view, filter, and manage all customer orders including advancing order status through its lifecycle, adding tracking information, writing admin notes, and issuing refunds.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-19: Manage orders*
- **Order list table**:
  - Filter orders by status (multi-select — can view multiple statuses at once) and by date range.
  - Displays: order number, customer info, total, status (color-coded badge), date.
  - Paginated with 25 rows per page (admin table pagination).
- **Order detail view**:
  - Shows everything the customer sees (items, shipping, payment summary, status timeline) plus:
    - Customer email address.
    - Admin notes field (editable, not visible to customers).
- **Status transitions**: Admins advance orders through the following workflow:
  - paid → processing → shipped → delivered.
  - When transitioning to "shipped", a tracking number is required.
  - Admins can also set status to "cancelled" or "refunded" as needed.
- **Refunds**:
  - Admins can issue refunds on any order that has been paid or completed (paid, processing, shipped, delivered).
  - Refund is processed via Stripe using the order's stripe_payment_intent_id.
  - Refunded orders have their status changed to "refunded".
- **Audit trail**: Every status change is logged with the admin's identity (user ID) and a timestamp.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **Order**: id, user_id (FK), order_number, status, shipping_address (JSON), shipping_method, shipping_cost, subtotal, tax_amount, total, tracking_number, estimated_delivery_date, stripe_payment_intent_id, admin_notes, created_at.
- **OrderItem**: id, order_id (FK), product_id, variant_id, product_name (snapshot), variant_name (snapshot), unit_price (snapshot), quantity, line_total.
- **OrderStatusLog**: id, order_id (FK), old_status, new_status, changed_by (FK — the admin user), timestamp. Every transition is recorded.
- **User**: Referenced for customer details (name, email) and for the admin identity in status logs.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Only admin users can access order management.
- Status transitions must follow the defined workflow — invalid transitions (e.g., delivered → processing) should be rejected.
- Tracking number is required when setting status to "shipped" — the transition should be rejected without it.
- Admin notes are only visible to admins, never to customers.
- Every status change must be logged in OrderStatusLog — this is mandatory for audit purposes.
- Out of scope for v1: partial refunds via admin (full refund only), returns and exchanges, email notifications to customers on status change, invoice/receipt PDF generation.

features.title: Admin user management
features.description:
Administrators can view and manage all user accounts, including promoting/demoting user roles and activating/deactivating accounts.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

*US-20: Manage user accounts*
- **User list table**:
  - Columns: email, full name, role (customer/admin), registration date, order count, active/inactive status.
  - Paginated with 25 rows per page (admin table pagination).
  - Searchable and sortable.
- **Role management**:
  - Admins can promote a customer to admin or demote an admin to customer.
  - **Safety constraint**: At least one admin account must always exist. The system must prevent demoting the last remaining admin.
- **Account activation/deactivation**:
  - Admins can deactivate a user account (set is_active = false).
  - Admins can reactivate a previously deactivated account (set is_active = true).
  - When a user is deactivated, they are forced to logout on their next API request (their tokens become invalid).
  - Deactivated accounts retain all data (orders, reviews, addresses) — this is a soft-delete, not a permanent deletion.
- **Scope of admin actions**: Admins can only change a user's role and active status. Admins cannot edit a user's profile details (name, email, phone, addresses).

**Data Model Entities:**

- **User**: id, email (unique), first_name, last_name, phone, role (customer | admin), is_active, created_at.
- **Order**: user_id (FK) — used to compute the order count displayed in the user table.
- **Address, Review, Wishlist, Cart**: All remain intact when a user is deactivated (soft-delete preserves all data).

**Scope Constraints:**
- Only admin users can access user management.
- The last admin cannot be demoted — the system must check and prevent this.
- Deactivation is a soft-delete: is_active = false. No user data is permanently removed.
- Deactivated users cannot log in and are forced out on their next request.
- Admins cannot modify profile fields — only role and active status.
- Out of scope for v1: account deletion, GDPR data export, multi-level admin permissions, customer-admin messaging.

features.title: Stock management (atomic decrement)
features.description:
Product inventory (stock) is tracked at the variant level and is decremented atomically during checkout to prevent overselling. Stock is validated at multiple points in the purchase flow.

**User Stories & Acceptance Criteria:**

Stock management is a cross-cutting concern referenced across multiple user stories:

*From US-4 (Product detail page):*
- Stock status is displayed per variant — shows "in stock" or "out of stock" based on stock_quantity.
- The "Add to Cart" button is disabled when the selected variant has stock_quantity = 0.

*From US-10 (Shopping cart):*
- Cart item quantity is capped at the variant's current stock_quantity.
- Stock is validated when viewing the cart (quantities adjusted if stock has changed).

*From US-11 (Checkout):*
- Stock is validated again at checkout before payment is processed.
- Upon successful payment, stock is decremented atomically using a conditional database update: `UPDATE ... SET stock_quantity = stock_quantity - $qty WHERE stock_quantity >= $qty`. This ensures the decrement only succeeds if sufficient stock exists.
- If the atomic decrement fails (insufficient stock), the checkout fails gracefully and the customer is informed.

*From US-15 (Order cancellation):*
- When an order is cancelled, stock quantities are restored (incremented back) for all items in the order.

*From US-17 (Admin product management):*
- Admins can view and edit stock quantities for each product variant.
- Total stock for a product is the sum of all its variant stock quantities.

**Data Model Entities:**

- **ProductVariant**: id, product_id (FK), stock_quantity (non-negative integer). This is the single source of truth for inventory. Stock is tracked per-variant, not per-product.
- **Product**: total_units_sold — incremented when orders are completed, used for popularity sorting and best-seller lists.
- **OrderItem**: quantity — the quantity purchased, used to determine the decrement amount.
- **CartItem**: quantity — validated against stock_quantity.

**Scope Constraints:**
- Stock quantity must never go negative. The atomic conditional update pattern (`WHERE stock_quantity >= $qty`) is the required approach.
- Stock is tracked per variant, not per product. A product's overall stock status is derived from its variants.
- Stock validation occurs at two mandatory points: when adding to cart and at checkout. The cart-time check is advisory (stock may change); the checkout-time check is authoritative.
- Stock restoration on cancellation must be reliable.
- Out of scope for v1: low-stock alerts, advanced inventory management, stock reservations/holds, backorder support, inventory history/audit log.
